title: The Queen of Air and Darkness
summary: The heroes chase Varga the Hutt to Cloud City on Bespin, infiltrate a Sabacc tournament to intercept an Imperial payment, and face a desperate hangar bay showdown that ends with an orbital battle and a moral choice that defines the party.

marks.label: Cloud City Welcome
marks.desc: Arrive on Bespin and establish your cover among the high-rollers and hustlers.

marks.label: The Game Within the Game
marks.desc: Enter the Sabacc tournament and use it to get close to the Imperial payment.

marks.label: The Contact
marks.desc: Identify and make contact with Arandis. Not everyone at the table is who they seem.

marks.label: The Intercept
marks.desc: Steal or redirect the Imperial payment before it reaches Varga's accounts.

marks.label: Hangar Bay Showdown
marks.desc: Fight your way through the hangar as the escape plan falls apart.

marks.label: Into the Clouds
marks.desc: Survive the orbital pursuit above Bespin. The tibanna gas clouds are your only cover.

marks.label: The Moral Ledger
marks.desc: Make the choice that defines who this crew really is. There's no taking it back.

marks.label: The Crucible
marks.desc: The plan catastrophically fails. Improvise under fire to survive.

gearNotes.reason: Varth coaches from the ship throughout — tournament strategy, Arandis identification, code decryption. Casino is crawling with Imperial undercover agents. Open comms = dead.
Commandeering the Glorious Chariot and Z-95s under fire with 30 seconds before a Stormtrooper platoon arrives. No time to improvise if something doesn't start cleanly.
Casino shootout into hangar battle into orbital combat — sustained multi-scene fighting with zero rest.
Catwalks over Bespin's atmosphere at Refinery Platform 13 and the open hangar bay. Safety line if someone gets knocked off — the drop is infinite.
Refinery recon ('they haven't seen you yet') and spotting Arandis's bodyguard positioning at the hotel.
Three weaponized dealer droids with hidden blaster cannons plus a heavy security droid at the hangar. Four droid encounters in one adventure.
Casino three-way firefight, city chase, and hangar assault — sightline control across three consecutive combat scenes.
Maintaining a convincing high-roller identity over a multi-day tournament. Also useful if Kessra from Adv2 shows up as hired muscle and might recognize a face.
Bugging Arandis's hotel conversations or his comms with the Stormtrooper detail.
Intercepting communications between Arandis and his handlers. Also useful during the chase to monitor Wing Guard frequencies.
Documenting Arandis's deliberate losing patterns as evidence of the Imperial payment scheme.
Sealed blast doors during the city chase if Varth's hack fails. Also cutting into the yacht if Varga seals the boarding ramp.
Hangar bay area denial against clustered pilots scrambling to Z-95 cockpits.
Casino surveillance avoidance when infiltrating as droid maintenance crew. Also useful during the Gauntlet chase.
Patching up between the casino firefight and the hangar assault. No time to rest.
Disabling the yacht's engines from outside if the heroes choose not to board.

scenes.title: The Brick Wall
scenes.readAloud:
Cloud City rises from the roiling orange clouds of Bespin like a crown of durasteel. In this era, it is not a polished resort — it is a rough-and-tumble mining boomtown, a Wild West in the clouds. Prospectors, gamblers, and fugitives pack the Cloudscape Platforms.

You hit the streets to find Varga — but the underworld is sealed tight. Every contact clams up. Then your datapads beep with an unsigned message:

"Stop digging. The drop is long, and the gas pressure is high. Go home."
scenes.gmNotes: Cloud City in 16 BBY is a mining boomtown, not Lando's resort. The heroes face a wall of silence — Varga has bribed the Wing Guard and port officials. The threatening message traces to the Parliament of Guilds (Ugnaught labor leadership), not Varga. Varth acts as 'The Man in the Chair,' analyzing data from the ship. The investigation leads to the Industrial District.

npcs.name: Local Contacts
npcs.notes: Tight-lipped. Varga has bribed everyone.

passive.name: Tight-Lipped
passive.description: Bribed or scared. Social checks to extract info are stepped up by 1 Power.

threatBuild.notes: Non-combatant social targets. Represent the wall of silence Varga has built.

scenes.hazards: Imperial spies. Varga's informant network. Wing Guard patrols.

decisions.consequence: Leads to the Ugnaught Parliament of Guilds in the Industrial District.
Risk attracting Varga's attention and Imperial surveillance.

environmentMechanics.name: Varga's Informant Network
environmentMechanics.effect: Street-level contacts are bribed. Aggressive questioning reaches Varga within hours.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Work through intermediaries. Don't mention Varga by name.

environmentMechanics.name: Wing Guard Patrols
environmentMechanics.effect: Some Wing Guards are on Varga's payroll. Will report unusual activity.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Blend in. Don't carry visible weapons in tourist areas.

rewards.narrative: Trail leads to the Ugnaught Parliament of Guilds in the Industrial District.

pacing.openingBeat: Cloud City. A mining boomtown, not a resort. Every contact clams up.
pacing.risingAction: The threatening message arrives. Investigation begins.
pacing.climax: The trail converges on the Ugnaught Works.

disciplineChallenges.context: Tracing the threatening message to its source.

control.failure: Message was routed through multiple proxies. Dead end on the digital trail. Whoever sent it knows their opsec.
control.mastery: Origin traced AND you recover fragments of the sender's other transmissions — they've been monitoring Varga's operations for months. This is an ongoing operation, not a one-off.

effect.fleeting: The encryption is commercial-grade, not military. Narrows it to Cloud City locals with access to public infrastructure.
effect.masterful: Digital forensics trace the origin to the Ugnaught Parliament of Guilds — an industrial administrator terminal. Someone with legitimate access.
effect.legendary: Full trace: the specific terminal, the time of transmission, and residual login credentials that point to a maintenance foreman named Fyren.

disciplineChallenges.context: Getting locals to talk about Varga. Everyone is bribed or scared.

control.failure: Stone wall. Nobody talks. Varga's money is louder than your charm.
control.mastery: The bartender not only directs you but slips you a hand-drawn map of the industrial levels where the Ugnaughts work. Marked with safe routes that avoid Varga's patrols.

effect.fleeting: A nervous glance toward the industrial district. Somebody almost talked before thinking better of it. You know the general direction.
effect.masterful: A bartender whispers: 'Try the Ugnaughts in the Works. They hate him more than they fear him.' First solid lead.
effect.legendary: The bartender gives you the Ugnaughts' names — Fyren and Krygg — their work shift, and the specific maintenance bay where they take breaks. Clean approach guaranteed.

disciplineChallenges.context: Moving through Cloud City without attracting Varga's informants.

control.failure: A street rat clocks you and runs toward the cantina district. Varga may know you're here within the hour.
control.mastery: Clean movement AND you identify two of Varga's informant positions. Can be avoided for the rest of the stay.

effect.fleeting: You stick to secondary corridors. Slower, but no obvious tails. Can't be sure you're clean though.
effect.masterful: Clean movement. No one flags your presence. Standard counter-surveillance protocols hold.
effect.legendary: Ghost-level movement. You map Varga's entire informant network on the tourist level as you move through it. Know exactly where the blind spots are for future operations.

disciplineChallenges.context: Creating a cover story for why you're on Cloud City.

control.failure: Cover is thin. First serious scrutiny will blow it. Don't get stopped by Wing Guard.
control.mastery: Cover is so good that Wing Guard actively assists you — offers directions and local guidance, treating you as welcome visitors.

effect.fleeting: Basic tourist cover. Won't survive a records check but it handles casual encounters.
effect.masterful: Convincing cover as prospectors or traders. Wing Guard wouldn't look twice.
effect.legendary: Airtight cover with planted digital records. If anyone checks, they find a legitimate business history, docking permits, and a Tibanna purchasing license.

disciplineChallenges.context: Reading the threatening message — is it a real threat or a warning?

control.failure: Reads as a genuine threat. Approach with caution — may be a trap.
control.mastery: Not just scared — the sender is watching the recipients right now. They're testing whether you're Varga's agents or potential allies.

effect.fleeting: The tone is off for a real threat. More nervous than menacing. Probably not an ambush.
effect.masterful: The tone is scared, not hostile. Whoever sent this is trying to protect themselves, not threaten you. A cry for help disguised as a warning.
effect.legendary: Specific linguistic patterns: industrial jargon, formal grammar, and a Ugnaught idiom translated into Basic. The sender is an educated Ugnaught trying to make contact safely.

scenes.title: The Industrial Connection
scenes.readAloud:
You descend into The Works — the noisy, steam-filled industrial bowels of Cloud City. The Ugnaughts who sent the threat aren't enemies; they're terrified administrators named Fyren and Krygg.

They admit they were trying to scare you away to prevent a gang war. They hate Varga. The Hutt is forcing Ugnaught riggers to siphon military-grade gas directly to Imperial ships, bypassing Guild quotas.

"Help us stop his thugs from stealing a shipment right now, and we'll tell you exactly how the handover at the tournament works."
scenes.gmNotes: The Ugnaughts are potential allies. They offer a trade: stop Varga's thugs from stealing a gas shipment at Refinery Platform 13, and they'll reveal the tournament handover structure. This establishes the Ugnaught labor exploitation angle and gives the heroes a clear objective.

npcs.name: Fyren
npcs.notes: Terrified but cooperative. Wants Varga gone.

passive.name: Guild Administrator
passive.description: Knows Cloud City's industrial infrastructure intimately. Can provide maps, schedules, and security gaps.

threatBuild.notes: Non-combatant ally. His value is information and infrastructure access.

npcs.name: Krygg
npcs.notes: More cautious than Fyren. Needs convincing.

passive.name: Cautious Pragmatist
passive.description: Krygg won't commit until he sees action. Once convinced, he's reliable.

threatBuild.notes: Non-combatant. More cautious than Fyren. Needs to see results before committing.

scenes.hazards: Industrial District — steam, noise, narrow catwalks. Imperial surveillance possible.

decisions.consequence: Leads to a tactical combat encounter at the refinery. Intel reward is worth it.
Ugnaughts refuse. They need proof the heroes are willing to act.

environmentMechanics.name: The Works
environmentMechanics.effect: Noisy industrial environment. Eavesdropping is nearly impossible — good for private conversations.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Use the noise as cover for planning.

items.name: Ugnaught Alliance
items.note: Safe houses and information network in Cloud City's Industrial District.

rewards.narrative: Deal struck with the Ugnaughts. Refinery Platform 13 objective established.

pacing.openingBeat: Descend into the Works. Steam, noise, and Ugnaught territory.
pacing.risingAction: Fyren and Krygg reveal the gas siphoning operation.
pacing.climax: The deal: stop the theft at the refinery, get the tournament intel.
pacing.resolution: Alliance formed. Heroes prep for the refinery.

disciplineChallenges.context: Convincing the Ugnaughts you're allies, not Varga's agents.

control.failure: They're not convinced. Demand proof of your intentions. May need to complete a task for them first.
control.mastery: Full trust earned immediately. They introduce you to the wider Ugnaught resistance network — six more workers who've been documenting Varga's operation.

effect.fleeting: Grudging willingness to talk. They'll share what they know but won't commit to anything further. Transactional.
effect.masterful: Fyren and Krygg open up. They describe the gas siphoning operation — scale, timing, and the Trandoshan enforcers who guard the refinery.
effect.legendary: Complete trust. They share everything: the siphoning operation, refinery layout, guard schedules, AND access codes for the industrial maintenance tunnels that bypass Varga's security.

disciplineChallenges.context: Reading Fyren and Krygg — are they trustworthy?

control.failure: Hard to read. Ugnaught body language is unfamiliar. Could be genuine or a setup.
control.mastery: Genuine — AND you notice Krygg keeps glancing at a datapad. He's been recording Varga's shipments. Physical evidence exists.

effect.fleeting: Their fear seems real. No signs of deception, but you can't be certain with alien body language.
effect.masterful: Genuine terror and anger. They've been exploited. This is real — and it's been going on for months.
effect.legendary: These two have been planning their own resistance for a year. You're not recruiting them — they've been waiting for someone exactly like you.

disciplineChallenges.context: Reviewing the Ugnaughts' evidence of Varga's gas siphoning.

control.failure: Evidence is circumstantial. Need more proof before acting.
control.mastery: The manifests show a destination code that matches Project Leviathan logistics. Direct connection between Varga's Tibanna and the Imperial superweapon project.

effect.fleeting: Numbers don't add up — more Tibanna is leaving Cloud City than the legitimate contracts account for. Something is being siphoned.
effect.masterful: Shipping manifests confirm military-grade Tibanna going to Imperial warships. The scale is staggering — this is industrial-level theft.
effect.legendary: Complete paper trail: production numbers, skimmed quantities, routing through shell companies, delivery coordinates, and Imperial authorization codes. Prosecution-quality evidence.

disciplineChallenges.context: Building rapport with the Ugnaughts for a deeper alliance.

control.failure: They'll trade info for the refinery job but nothing more. Purely transactional.
control.mastery: Deep alliance formed. The Ugnaughts offer their maintenance access tunnels as a permanent safe network throughout Cloud City. Game-changing mobility advantage.

effect.fleeting: Friendly but cautious. They'll help with the refinery but need to see results before committing further.
effect.masterful: They commit fully. Will provide ongoing support — safe houses, equipment, information. A real alliance.
effect.legendary: Blood oath (Ugnaught tradition). They pledge their guild's resources: safe houses, fabrication equipment, encrypted comm network, and a dedicated guide for the industrial levels.

scenes.title: The Refinery Rumble
scenes.readAloud:
Refinery Platform 13 hangs deep in the gas giant's atmosphere. The wind howls across exposed catwalks, and steam vents obscure your vision. Below, there is nothing — just the infinite amber depths of Bespin.

On the platform, Trandoshan Mercenaries and corrupt Ugnaught riggers are loading gas canisters onto a shuttle. They haven't seen you yet.
scenes.gmNotes: Tactical combat on catwalks over a lethal drop. Stray shots risk rupturing gas tanks (explosions) or steam pipes (scald damage). The objective is to defeat the Trandoshans and secure the platform. A datapad on the Trandoshan leader confirms the gas is destined for the Imperial Navy and mentions a ship called the 'Shackles of Nizon.'

npcs.name: Trandoshan Mercenaries
npcs.notes: Varga's muscle. Loading stolen gas.

npcEffects.legendary: Chassis damage + [Staggered] + [Prone]

maneuver.name: Intimidating Advance
maneuver.description: Modifies Move. Move 1 zone toward a target. That target must succeed on a Grit check vs Threat's Power or gain [Rattled].

gambit.name: Brutal Strike
gambit.description: In addition to normal effects, target gains [Staggered] Tactical.

exploit.name: Predator's Opening
exploit.trigger: A PC in the same zone takes damage from another source this round.
exploit.description: Immediately make an attack against that PC at -2 Power.

threatBuild.notes: Same Trandoshan build as Adv2. On catwalks, their Knockdown gambit is potentially lethal — [Prone] near an edge.

npcs.name: Corrupt Ugnaught Riggers
npcs.notes: Non-combatants who flee if fighting starts.

passive.name: Coerced Workers
passive.description: These Ugnaughts are forced labor, not fighters. They flee immediately when combat starts.

threatBuild.notes: Non-combatant. Will surrender or flee. Questioning them provides limited intel about Varga's operation.

scenes.hazards: Catwalks over a lethal drop. Gas tank explosions. Steam pipe scald damage. High winds.

decisions.consequence: Element of surprise. Risk of gas tank explosions in crossfire.

decisions.choice: Try to disable the shuttle first
decisions.consequence: Prevents escape but alerts the Trandoshans. Harder initial engagement.

encounters.name: Refinery Platform Ambush
encounters.description: 5 Trandoshan Mercenaries loading stolen Tibanna gas. Fight on catwalks over Bespin's atmosphere.
encounters.tactics: Trandoshans use gas canisters as cover. Shooting canisters causes explosions. Catwalks are narrow — no flanking.

environmentMechanics.name: Lethal Drop
environmentMechanics.effect: No ground beneath the catwalks — only Bespin's atmosphere. Any character knocked off a catwalk falls to their death unless caught.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Syntherope safety lines. Stay away from edges.

environmentMechanics.name: High Winds
environmentMechanics.effect: Athletics check at start of each round or [Slowed]. Ranged attacks at long range suffer -1 Power.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Close range combat. Melee is unaffected.

environmentMechanics.name: Tibanna Gas Tanks
environmentMechanics.effect: Explosion: [Hazard 3] in the zone. Destroys that section of catwalk.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Don't shoot near the tanks. Or do, tactically.

environmentMechanics.name: Steam Vents
environmentMechanics.effect: Random venting. Characters in zone gain [Concealment] but also [Hazard 1].
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Use for cover. Accept the minor burn.

items.name: Trandoshan Datapad
items.note: References "Shackles of Nizon" and Imperial Navy gas deliveries.

rewards.narrative: Refinery secured. Ugnaught deal honored. Tournament intel incoming.

pacing.openingBeat: The platform hangs in Bespin's atmosphere. Trandoshans loading gas below.
pacing.risingAction: The ambush. Fighting on catwalks in wind and steam.
pacing.climax: The shuttle — stop it from escaping with the gas.

disciplineChallenges.context: Approaching the refinery platform undetected.

control.failure: Spotted at the platform edge. Trandoshans scramble to defensive positions. No surprise round.
control.mastery: Perfect approach AND you identify the sentry rotation gap — 90 seconds when the entire east platform is unmonitored. Sabotage window.

effect.fleeting: Close approach but a sentry turns. You freeze in time — they didn't see you, but the window is narrow. One chance.
effect.masterful: Perfect approach. Element of surprise secured. Full ambush positioning available.
effect.legendary: You're inside the refinery perimeter before anyone knows. Close enough to sabotage equipment, plant charges, or pick off sentries one at a time.

disciplineChallenges.context: Ambush fire on the Trandoshan loading crew.

control.failure: Shots go wide in the wind. Trandoshans return fire immediately. No surprise advantage.
control.mastery: First volley drops the crew leader. The remaining Trandoshans panic — leaderless, they fight defensively instead of aggressively.

effect.fleeting: One shot connects but it's a grazing hit. The Trandoshans know you're there — surprise is spent but they're not organized yet.
effect.masterful: First volley drops one Trandoshan. Others scatter for cover. The ambush lands clean.
effect.legendary: Devastating opening volley. Two Trandoshans drop before anyone fires back. The survivors are pinned behind Tibanna crates — which they can't return fire through without risking detonation.

disciplineChallenges.context: Fighting on narrow catwalks in high winds.

control.failure: Narrow catwalk. Slippery footing. Must slow down or risk falling.
control.mastery: You vault the machinery so fast you end up behind the Trandoshan line. They're flanked.

effect.fleeting: Careful movement across the grated platform. Slow but steady. No falls.
effect.masterful: Confident movement. Use the machinery and catwalks to close distance and gain positional advantage.
effect.legendary: Acrobatic traversal — you use pipes, railings, and machinery housings to cross the refinery like a gymnast. Reach any position in the facility in a single round.

disciplineChallenges.context: Disabling the shuttle to prevent the Trandoshans from escaping with the gas.

control.failure: Partial shutdown. Production slows but doesn't stop. Varga will notice within hours, not days.
control.mastery: Clean shutdown AND you plant a data siphon on the control systems. Future operations will provide real-time production data.

effect.fleeting: Emergency shutdown triggered. The refinery stops but the restart sequence is automated — it'll be back online within a day.
effect.masterful: Clean shutdown with safety lockouts engaged. Requires a qualified engineer to restart — Varga loses days of production.
effect.legendary: Precision sabotage. You don't just shut it down — you introduce subtle calibration errors that will cause the next batch of Tibanna to be worthless. Varga won't know until the shipment is tested.

disciplineChallenges.context: Searching the Trandoshan leader's datapad after the fight.

control.failure: Datapad is damaged. Partial data only — fragments of shipping schedules.
control.mastery: Full data AND you discover a shipping code that matches Project Leviathan logistics manifests from Adv1. The Tibanna fuels the superweapon.

effect.fleeting: Fragments of delivery schedules. Gas is going to Imperial ships, but destination details are corrupted.
effect.masterful: Gas is destined for the Imperial Navy. Mentions 'Shackles of Nizon' — a slave transport ship making regular runs.
effect.legendary: Complete shipping network: routes, schedules, quantities, and the Shackles of Nizon as the primary bulk carrier. Enough intel to track or intercept future shipments.

scenes.title: The Imperial Mark
scenes.readAloud:
The Ugnaughts pay their debt. The handover isn't happening in secret — Varga insisted it occur in plain sight during the Cloud City Sabacc Tournament.

At the Yerith Bespin Hotel, you spot the Imperial Bagman: Lieutenant Armen Arandis. Dressed in civilian luxury but moving with military stiffness, flanked by 'bodyguards' who are clearly undercover Stormtroopers.

Varth's voice crackles in your ear: "That's the Bagman. He isn't here to play cards — he's here to transfer credits to Varga's proxy by losing intentionally. We need to be at that table."
scenes.gmNotes: Arandis is an Imperial math genius posing as a gambler. His job is to lose the pot intentionally to Varga's proxy. The heroes need to infiltrate the tournament to intercept the credits. The buy-in is massive (10,000 credits) — Varth provides the stake. Heroes must decide roles: The Player (gambler), The Security (hired guards), The Cheat (droid maintenance infiltrator).

npcs.name: Lt. Armen Arandis
npcs.notes: Mathematical genius. Cold, emotionless. Here to lose intentionally.

npcEffects.masterful: Chassis damage + one ally in adjacent zone may move 1 zone toward target
npcEffects.legendary: Chassis damage + ally moves 1 zone + that ally gains [Optimized]

maneuver.name: Regroup
maneuver.description: Modifies Coordinate. Choose one ally within 2 zones — that ally immediately moves 1 zone (Leader's choice). Diceless.

gambit.name: Outflank
gambit.description: In addition to normal effects, target gains [Exposed] Tactical. Coordinated positioning strips cover.

exploit.name: Tactical Withdrawal
exploit.trigger: An ally in the Leader-Harrier's zone drops below half Vitality.
exploit.description: That ally immediately moves 1 zone (no Opening).

threatBuild.notes: Operative. Disciplined: +1 Resist. Arandis is a genius pretending to be a gambler. His death triggers an alert to Draco.

npcs.name: Undercover Stormtroopers
npcs.notes: Arandis's bodyguards. Professional but trying to blend in.

npcEffects.legendary: Chassis damage + [Suppressed] to all enemies in the target's zone

maneuver.name: Dig In
maneuver.description: Modifies Take Cover. Anchor gains [Cover 2] and [Optimized] on its next ranged attack. Cannot move this turn.

gambit.name: Pinning Barrage
gambit.description: In addition to normal effects, target gains [Suppressed] Tactical. Target cannot use Move next turn.

exploit.name: Covering Fire
exploit.trigger: An ally in the Anchor's zone or an adjacent zone takes damage.
exploit.description: Immediately make an attack at -2 Power against the attacker if in weapon range.

threatBuild.notes: Standard Stormtroopers in civilian disguise. No armor = reduced Defense. Same training, less protection.

scenes.hazards: Heavy hotel security. Imperial surveillance. High-profile public venue.

decisions.consequence: Direct access to the table. Requires gambling skill.
Free movement but no access to the game itself.
Can rig dealer droids. High-tech approach.

environmentMechanics.name: Hotel Security
environmentMechanics.effect: Professional security. Cameras, badge checks, restricted floors.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Legitimate entry as tournament player or staff.

environmentMechanics.name: Imperial Surveillance
environmentMechanics.effect: Undercover Stormtroopers monitor all approaches. Counter-surveillance active.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Don't approach Arandis directly. Observe from distance.

rewards.narrative: Tournament roles assigned. The heist is planned.

pacing.openingBeat: The Ugnaughts deliver: Varga's payment happens at the Sabacc tournament.
pacing.risingAction: Arandis is identified. His bodyguards are Stormtroopers. Stakes escalate.
pacing.climax: The heroes choose their tournament roles.
pacing.resolution: Plan is set. Buy-in secured. The game begins.

disciplineChallenges.context: Identifying Arandis's bodyguards as undercover Stormtroopers.

control.failure: They look like professional bodyguards. Nothing unusual stands out.
control.mastery: Stormtroopers AND you identify the specific unit insignia hidden under their civilian clothes — 501st Legion detachment. Elite soldiers, not standard garrison.

effect.fleeting: Something off about their posture — too rigid, too disciplined for hired muscle. Military bearing, but you can't confirm more.
effect.masterful: Military bearing, identical boots, comm earpieces. Stormtroopers in civilian clothes. This is an Imperial operation.
effect.legendary: Full identification: Stormtrooper unit, comm frequencies they're using, and the fact that they're reporting to someone not in the room. Arandis has a handler.

disciplineChallenges.context: Creating a tournament entry identity — the Player persona.

control.failure: Minor discrepancy noted. Tournament staff may verify during play. A ticking clock.
control.mastery: Cover is flawless AND the tournament organizers seat you at a premium table — prime position to observe Arandis during play.

effect.fleeting: Entry accepted with a raised eyebrow. The cover holds for now but don't draw attention.
effect.masterful: Tournament entry secured cleanly. No flags raised. Standard participant access.
effect.legendary: The Player persona is so convincing that other participants approach with deference. You're treated as a known quantity — a high-roller from the Outer Rim. Doors open.

disciplineChallenges.context: Casing the Yerith Bespin Hotel without being noticed.

control.failure: Hotel security notices your loitering. You're remembered. Future visits will be flagged.
control.mastery: Full layout mapped AND you identify a service entrance that bypasses all security checkpoints. Direct access to the tournament floor.

effect.fleeting: Basic layout observed from public areas. You know the lobby, elevators, and main entrance. Cameras are visible but blind spots aren't mapped.
effect.masterful: Full layout mapped. Security camera blind spots identified. Guard rotation patterns timed. Standard casino reconnaissance.
effect.legendary: Complete security profile: camera blind spots, guard rotations, service tunnels, emergency exits, and the master security office location. You could rob this place.

disciplineChallenges.context: Infiltrating the casino's droid maintenance network.

control.failure: Firewalled. Need physical access to a maintenance terminal in the restricted wing.
control.mastery: Full access AND you discover the casino's surveillance feed. Can monitor any table from a remote terminal — watch Arandis, Fioro, and Varga's proxy in real time.

effect.fleeting: Partial access. You can see the droid maintenance schedule but can't modify anything. Still useful for timing.
effect.masterful: Access to dealer droid firmware. Can monitor or modify card dealing. The tournament can be influenced from the shadows.
effect.legendary: Root access. You own the casino's droid network — dealing, serving, cleaning droids all respond to your commands. Unprecedented control over the tournament environment.

disciplineChallenges.context: Studying Arandis's playing pattern over surveillance.

control.failure: She seems like what she claims — a wealthy patron enjoying the tournament.
control.mastery: She's conflicted. Whatever her mission is, she doesn't fully believe in it. There's a moral crack that could be exploited.

effect.fleeting: Something rehearsed about her mannerisms. She's playing a role, but you can't tell which part is the mask.
effect.masterful: She's watching Varga's representative as much as she's watching the game. This isn't leisure — it's surveillance.
effect.legendary: Full read: Imperial operative on an intelligence-gathering mission, uncomfortable with the criminal elements she's embedded with, and personally conflicted about her assignment.

scenes.title: The Buy-In
scenes.subtitle: Preparation
scenes.readAloud:
As you finalize your entry strategy, Varth's voice cuts in — tight with genuine fear.

"We have a problem. A massive energy spike just hit the system edge. An Imperial Star Destroyer just dropped out of hyperspace. It's the Assiduous — Inquisitor Draco's flagship."

A beat of silence.

"That ship is the clean-up crew. If Arandis loses the money wrong, or if Varga double-crosses them, Draco won't bother with arrests. He'll glass the city to bury the evidence. Win that pot and get the codes before Draco gets impatient."
scenes.gmNotes: The Assiduous arrives — escalating the stakes dramatically. Draco is the 'clean-up crew' for Project Leviathan. If the deal goes wrong, he'll destroy Cloud City to bury the evidence. This puts a hard clock on the entire tournament arc. The heroes must win the pot and get the gas codes before Draco loses patience.

npcs.name: Inquisitor Valin Draco
npcs.notes: Aboard the Assiduous in orbit. Prepared to 'glass the city' if needed.

npcEffects.legendary: Chassis damage + [Suppressed] + Controller's zone becomes [Hazard 1] until end of next round

maneuver.name: Overwatch Position
maneuver.description: Modifies Overwatch. Declare a zone. Any PC that enters, leaves, or takes an action in that zone presents an Opening AND gains [Suppressed].

gambit.name: Disrupt
gambit.description: In addition to normal effects, target gains [Dazed] Tactical. Target loses their Maneuver next turn.

exploit.name: Denial Fire
exploit.trigger: A PC enters a zone the Controller has declared as Overwatch.
exploit.description: Immediately attack that PC at -2 Power.

control.failure: Plan has gaps. Improvisation will be needed. High risk of unexpected variables.
control.mastery: Perfect plan AND contingencies for every failure point. Each team member has a fallback role if their primary assignment goes sideways.

effect.masterful: Clear plan with contingencies. Each team member knows their role, their fallback, and the extraction signal.

disciplineChallenges.context: Scanning the system for the Assiduous's capabilities.

control.failure: Confirm it's an ISD. Details unclear. You know it's dangerous — how dangerous is the question.
control.mastery: Full profile AND you identify the Star Destroyer's scanning patterns — blind spots in its sensor sweep you can exploit during the escape.

effect.fleeting: Imperial Star Destroyer confirmed. Standard armament, standard complement. Bad news but not unexpected.
effect.masterful: Star Destroyer identified. Full sensor profile. Draco is aboard — confirmed by command frequencies. TIE complement estimated.
effect.legendary: Complete tactical analysis: shield frequency, turbolaser arcs, TIE launch timing, and — critically — the Assiduous has a damaged port sensor array. Blind spot on the port quarter.

disciplineChallenges.context: Staying focused despite the escalation — Star Destroyer in orbit.

control.failure: [Rattled] from the weight of the stakes. -1 Power on first tournament check. The fear is real.
control.mastery: Absolute ice. The Star Destroyer in orbit focuses you instead of scaring you. Every action from here on out is precise and deliberate.

effect.fleeting: Deep breath. Hands steady. You can operate, but the shadow of that Star Destroyer is always in the back of your mind.
effect.masterful: Steel nerves. The plan holds. You acknowledge the danger and move forward with professional detachment.
effect.legendary: The danger sharpens everything. Colors are brighter, sounds are clearer, reactions are faster. You're operating at your absolute peak because failure isn't an option.

scenes.title: The Elimination Rounds
scenes.subtitle: Days 1 & 2
scenes.readAloud:
The Royal Casino is a loud, smoky, aggressive establishment where prospectors gamble away fortunes and refugees bet their last credits. Over fifty competitors crowd the tables. The air is thick with Corellian whiskey, Ryll spice smoke, and the mechanical whir of shuffling droids.

You notice two players worth watching: a flamboyant human named Silas Draver who seems drunk but isn't, and a cold Hapan noblewoman named Lady Fioro who bankrupts a weeping Dug in the first hour.
scenes.gmNotes:
Days 1-2 of the tournament. The heroes identify key players:
- Lt. Arandis at Table 4: plays like a machine. Folds strong hands, raises weak ones against specific opponents.
- Silas Draver: loud and arrogant but stone cold sober. Casing the room and checking camera sightlines. He's planning a heist.
- Lady Mirana Fioro: icy Hapan noblewoman. Keeps glancing at Arandis with calculation — she's Varga's proxy.

Varth coaches via comms: 'Watch who Arandis loses to. He's not allowed to lose to anyone but Varga's proxy.'

npcs.name: Silas Draver
npcs.notes: Posing as a drunk gambler. Actually casing the room for a robbery.

pacing.openingBeat: The Royal Casino. Fifty players. Smoke and tension.
pacing.risingAction: Days 1-2 of play. Players identified. Patterns emerge.

pacing.resolution: Advancement to the next round. Intel gathered.

disciplineChallenges.context: Playing Sabacc — maintaining a poker face while reading opponents.

control.failure: Your poker face slips. An observant opponent reads your tell. You're at a disadvantage for the rest of the round.
control.mastery: Your performance is so convincing that opponents misread your strategy entirely. They play into your hands without knowing it.

effect.fleeting: Basic composure maintained. Nobody reads your hand, but you don't learn much from them either.
effect.masterful: Poker face holds perfectly. You project false confidence on weak hands and false uncertainty on strong ones. Opponents are off-balance.
effect.legendary: You control the entire table's perception. Every bluff lands, every trap springs. Opponents fold when you want them to and bet when you want them to. The elimination rounds become a performance.

disciplineChallenges.context: Reading other players — identifying Varga's proxy.

control.failure: Too many players to read. Can't identify the connection. Everyone's wearing a poker face.
control.mastery: Fioro identified AND you notice she's passing information via the dealer droid. She's using the casino's own system as a dead drop.

effect.fleeting: One player seems unusually interested in the tournament results beyond gambling. Could be Varga's proxy or just a serious competitor.
effect.masterful: Lady Fioro keeps glancing at Arandis. She's the proxy — managing Varga's interests at the table.
effect.legendary: Complete social map: Fioro is Varga's proxy, she's watching Arandis, and there's a third player who's an independent operator — Draver — with his own agenda for the tournament.

disciplineChallenges.context: Analyzing Arandis's playing pattern between hands.

control.failure: Too many shell companies. The financial trail loops back on itself.
control.mastery: Full network mapped AND you discover Skreev's identity: Varga's chief intelligence operative, embedded in Cloud City's Wing Guard as a lieutenant.

effect.fleeting: A pattern emerges — three shell companies all registered to the same address. Getting warmer.
effect.masterful: Shell company network traced to a single operator: codename Skreev. Varga's intelligence chief on Cloud City. Real identity unknown.
effect.legendary: Complete dossier on Skreev: real name, Wing Guard rank, personal residence on Cloud City, known associates, and communication frequencies. The hunter becomes the hunted.

disciplineChallenges.context: Slipping away from the table to investigate Draver's activities.

control.failure: You're being followed. Skreev knows you're investigating. The Knife in the Dark accelerates.
control.mastery: Not only undetected but you identify and photograph Skreev's surveillance team. Can be used as leverage or to set a counter-trap.

effect.fleeting: Careful movement. You think you're clean but can't be certain. No obvious tails.
effect.masterful: Clean counter-surveillance. Skreev's watchers don't know you're investigating him.
effect.legendary: You turn the surveillance back on them. Every watcher is identified, photographed, and their patrol patterns logged. Skreev's network is compromised without him knowing.

disciplineChallenges.context: Building rapport with other players to gather intel.

control.failure: Other players are guarded. Tournament atmosphere is tense. Small talk bounces off.
control.mastery: A talkative Bothan reveals Fioro's ship registration AND mentions that a 'scary Zabrak' visited Fioro's hotel room last night. Skreev is in contact with the proxy.

effect.fleeting: Polite conversation but nothing of substance. Everyone's playing it close.
effect.masterful: A talkative Bothan reveals: Fioro arrived on a ship registered to Varga's company. Confirmed proxy.
effect.legendary: Multiple players open up. You learn Fioro's background (ex-Imperial bureaucrat turned fixer), her hotel room number, and the fact that she takes a specific service corridor to avoid cameras.

scenes.title: The Pressure Cooker
scenes.subtitle: Day 3
scenes.readAloud:
Before the tables open on Day 3, you catch Maya and Varth in a hushed argument near the docking bay. Maya's voice is low but furious:

"There are people on that ship, Varth. Living people in slave circuits. We have the datapad. We know where the Shackles of Nizon docks. We could —"

Varth cuts her off, cold and final: "That is not why we are here. We are here for the money. A rescue operation against an Imperial slave transport is suicide, and I will not risk this crew on your conscience."

Maya stares at him. Then she walks away without another word.

Day three. Only two tables remain. The buy-ins have escalated, and the audience has shifted from celebration to funeral silence.

Midway through the session, a Devaronian gambler named Szygar goes bust against Arandis. He snaps, leaping up and screaming accusations of cheating. A hold-out blaster appears in his hand.

Panic ripples through the crowd. Arandis doesn't flinch.
scenes.gmNotes:
OPENING BEAT: The heroes overhear Maya and Varth arguing about the Shackles of Nizon slave transport (from the Trandoshan datapad in Part 1). Maya wants to intervene and free the slaves. Varth shuts her down — too risky, not the mission. This is critical foreshadowing: Maya cares enough to act. When she later goes to the slave ship on her own and Varth conveniently never tells the crew, the players will remember this moment and understand what he allowed to happen.

The Szygar incident is a test for the heroes. If working security, they must subdue him without causing a casino shutdown. If playing, they must decide whether to intervene. If heroes don't act, Arandis's undercover Stormtroopers will vaporize Szygar, potentially causing a police investigation that cancels the tournament. Varth: 'Don't let them shoot! If the cops shut us down, we lose the money!' Success earns trust from casino owner Jacc Mandelbrot.

decisions.consequence: Earns trust from Mandelbrot. Casino stays open. Tournament continues.
They kill Szygar. Potential police investigation. Risk of tournament cancellation.

narrativeLinks.note: The Trandoshan datapad from the Refinery Rumble is what Maya is referencing — she read about the Shackles of Nizon and wants to act.
Maya goes to the Shackles of Nizon on her own — because Varth told her no and she couldn't let it go. Varth knew she went and never told the crew. He let it happen.

pacing.openingBeat: Maya and Varth argue about the slave ship. She wants to help. He refuses. Then Day 3 begins.

disciplineChallenges.context: Subduing Szygar without lethal force.

control.failure: Szygar fires wildly. Panic erupts. Security responds. The tournament may be suspended.
control.mastery: Szygar subdued so quickly and quietly that no one outside the immediate area even notices. No security response. Tournament continues.

control.failure: Szygar is beyond reason. 'You don't understand — they'll kill my family!' Physical intervention needed.
control.mastery: Szygar not only stands down but provides intel: he was forced to cheat by 'a Zabrak who knows things.' Skreev's involvement confirmed.

effect.fleeting: Szygar pauses. The blaster lowers half an inch. He's listening but he's still terrified. More talking needed.
effect.masterful: Szygar lowers the blaster. Breaks down crying. Quiet resolution. He's a victim, not a villain.
effect.legendary: Complete de-escalation. Szygar surrenders the blaster, sits down, and tells you everything — who blackmailed him, what they wanted, and where the handler is staying.

disciplineChallenges.context: Winning a critical Sabacc hand on Day 3.

control.failure: Lose the hand. Must find another way into the final round — or another path to the objective.
control.mastery: Win the hand AND your performance is so convincing that Fioro approaches afterward — she wants to make a deal.

effect.fleeting: Narrow win. The other players suspect something but can't prove it. You advance, barely.
effect.masterful: Win. Clean performance. Advance to the final table with reputation intact.
effect.legendary: Dominating victory that looks effortless. You're the story of the round. Fioro, Arandis, and every observer are watching your next move.

disciplineChallenges.context: Reading Szygar — is he genuinely dangerous or just desperate?

control.failure: He looks unhinged. Could go either way. Dangerous to approach.
control.mastery: Desperate AND you read the specific fear: his family is being held. The leverage isn't money — it's hostages. Skreev's operation is darker than expected.

effect.fleeting: More scared than angry. He's holding that blaster like it's the only thing keeping him alive.
effect.masterful: Desperate. Not a killer. The blaster is shaking. He's been pushed into this by someone else.
effect.legendary: Full read: terrified father, coerced into cheating, handler watching from somewhere in the casino. He'll stand down if you can guarantee his family's safety.

disciplineChallenges.context: Commanding Szygar to drop the weapon.

control.failure: Szygar panics harder. The blaster swings wildly. Situation worsens.
control.mastery: Szygar drops the blaster immediately AND tells you who gave it to him. Direct lead to Skreev's operation.

effect.fleeting: Szygar flinches but doesn't drop the weapon. He's caught between two fears.
effect.masterful: Szygar's resolve crumbles. Blaster hits the floor. He puts his hands up. Over.
effect.legendary: Total submission. Szygar collapses. He's so terrified of you that he forgets to be afraid of Skreev. Full cooperation from pure fear.

scenes.title: The Final Table
scenes.subtitle: Day 4
scenes.readAloud:
The final day. Five players remain under a harsh spotlight: Arandis, Fioro, Draver, the Rodian Creeska, and you.

Arandis plays strangely — folding strong hands against everyone except Fioro. He goes All In against her. She calls.

He reveals a losing hand. The crowd erupts. But instead of sweeping his cards away, Arandis deliberately arranges them on the table: One of Coins. Three of Sabers. Seven of Flasks.

Varth decodes it instantly: "1-3-7. That's a frequency — 137.12. It's the slave circuit control code for the Shackles of Nizon. He just handed her the keys to the getaway car AND the money."
scenes.gmNotes: The climax of the game. Arandis deliberately loses the massive pot to Fioro (Varga's proxy), transferring the Imperial payment. The card arrangement is a code — the slave circuit frequency for the Shackles of Nizon. Fioro now has the credits (digitally transferred to a datapad) and the ship code. The heroes must grab her before she leaves. This is the moment the heist transitions to action.

control.mastery: Full account access AND you discover a hidden sub-account routing credits to an Imperial officer — Varth. Direct evidence of the embezzlement pipeline.

effect.masterful: Full account access. Transaction history shows Tibanna payments routing to Imperial logistics. Complete evidence chain.

effect.legendary: Full psychological read: Fioro is a pragmatist trapped in a crime syndicate. She has more intelligence than she's revealed and will share it all if she believes you can protect her.

effect.masterful: Clean pickpocket. Fioro's datapad is in your hands and a decoy is in hers. She won't notice for hours.
effect.legendary: Perfect swap executed during a handshake. The decoy datapad feeds Fioro false data while you analyze the real one. She's unknowingly working with compromised intel.

scenes.title: The Heist
scenes.subtitle: Combat
scenes.readAloud:
Just as you move to intercept Fioro, Silas Draver makes his play. He stands, pulls a heavy blaster, and fires into the ceiling.

"Nobody moves! The Empire isn't the only one getting paid today!"

The dealer droids spin around, revealing hidden blaster cannons. Draver's men — disguised as waiters and guests — pull weapons and secure the exits. Draver grabs Fioro, pressing a blaster to her temple.

"The datapad. Now."
scenes.gmNotes: Three-way crossfire in a crowded casino. Draver weaponized the dealer droids and planted his men as staff. The heroes are caught between Draver's heist crew, panicking civilians, and Arandis who draws a concealed military blaster to protect the transaction's secrecy. Arandis likely dies in the crossfire, removing the Imperial witness. Varth: 'Forget the civilians! Get the datapad!' After defeating Draver, Fioro spills Varga's location: Docking Bay 4414.

decisions.choice: Grab the datapad in the chaos
decisions.consequence: May not get Varga's location. But secures the credits.

encounters.name: Casino Three-Way Firefight
encounters.description: Draver's thugs, weaponized droids, and Arandis's Stormtroopers all in a crowded casino. Heroes caught in the middle.
encounters.tactics: Draver focuses on Fioro and the datapad. Arandis fights to protect the transaction's secrecy. Heroes must secure Fioro and the intel.

environmentMechanics.name: Three-Way Crossfire
environmentMechanics.effect: Three factions firing simultaneously. Each round, stray shots hit random targets.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Use overturned tables as cover. Stay low.

environmentMechanics.name: Panicking Civilians
environmentMechanics.effect: Stampeding crowd. Difficult terrain. Risk of trampling damage.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Move against the flow or use maintenance exits.

environmentMechanics.name: Weaponized Dealer Droids
environmentMechanics.effect: Three flying droids with hidden blasters. 360-degree fire arcs. Ion weapons are ideal.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Ion grenades. Tech hack. Or concentrated fire.

items.name: Fioro's Datapad
items.note: If secured — contains credits and Varga's docking bay location.

rewards.narrative: Casino heist resolved. Varga's location revealed. The chase begins.

pacing.openingBeat: Moving to intercept Fioro — then Draver strikes.
pacing.risingAction: Three-way crossfire erupts. Chaos in the casino.
pacing.climax: Free Fioro from Draver. Secure the datapad.
pacing.resolution: Arandis dies in the crossfire. Fioro spills Varga's location.

disciplineChallenges.context: Firefight in the casino during Draver's heist.

control.failure: Crossfire pins you down. Three factions shooting in a casino full of civilians. Chaos.
control.mastery: Precision shots drop two of Draver's crew. His heist collapses. He's exposed and desperate — which makes him predictable.

effect.fleeting: Suppressive fire keeps Draver's crew from advancing. Civilians scatter. Some cover established.
effect.masterful: Accurate fire. One of Draver's crew drops. The three-way firefight tilts in your favor.
effect.legendary: Controlled violence in a chaotic environment. You drop targets without hitting civilians, use the casino furniture for cover, and create a corridor of safety for bystanders to escape.

disciplineChallenges.context: Dodging crossfire between three factions.

control.failure: Hit by glancing fire from an unknown direction. Minor wound. The crossfire is disorienting.
control.mastery: You use the crossfire to your advantage — dive through the gap between two firing lines and emerge behind Draver's position.

effect.fleeting: Dive behind a sabacc table. Chips scatter. The bolts hit the wall behind where you were standing.
effect.masterful: Clean dodge through the crossfire. You reach solid cover and a clear firing position.
effect.legendary: Acrobatic evasion through the three-way kill zone. You roll, slide, and vault your way through the casino without taking a single hit. End up exactly where you need to be.

disciplineChallenges.context: Disabling the weaponized dealer droids.

control.failure: Circuit triggers a pain response. The slaves scream. Must work faster before permanent damage.
control.mastery: Clean override AND you capture the master frequency. Can disable any slave circuit on this frequency band — every Iron Ring slave in range.

effect.fleeting: One circuit disabled. Painstaking, delicate work. Each one takes time you may not have.
effect.masterful: Batch override. Multiple circuits disabled simultaneously. The slaves stir, disoriented but free.
effect.legendary: Master override. Every slave circuit on the ship deactivates at once. Clean, painless, complete. The freed slaves look at you like you're a miracle.

disciplineChallenges.context: Managing the three-way crossfire.

control.failure: Chaotic scene. React to threats as they emerge. No clear picture.
control.mastery: You identify a way to collapse the three-way into a two-on-one: maneuver Draver's crew into the security team's line of fire. Let them fight each other.

effect.fleeting: Identify the three factions and their positions. Enough to know where NOT to stand.
effect.masterful: Full tactical picture: Draver at 2 o'clock, casino security at 10, your team in between. Clear firing lanes identified.
effect.legendary: Complete battle management. You see how to end this in two rounds: pin Draver between security and your team, isolate Fioro, and grab the datapad in the confusion.

disciplineChallenges.context: Disarming Draver to free Fioro.

control.failure: Draver tightens his grip. Hostage situation escalates. He drags Fioro toward the exit.
control.mastery: Draver disarmed AND unconscious. Fioro is freed AND grateful — she'll cooperate fully from this point.

effect.fleeting: You loosen Draver's grip but Fioro is still in the struggle. Partial progress.
effect.masterful: Draver loses the blaster. Fioro is freed. Clean disarm.
effect.legendary: One precise strike to Draver's wrist — nerve cluster hit. His hand opens involuntarily. Fioro stumbles free. Draver is done before he realizes what happened.

scenes.title: The Aftermath
scenes.readAloud:
The casino is a wreck. Sirens wail in the distance. Lady Fioro, wounded and terrified, spills everything.

"He's at the lower docks — Docking Bay 4414. He has the Shackles of Nizon holding in orbit on slave circuits. He's taking the gas and the slaves and leaving the sector tonight!"

Varth scans the system. His voice is grim: "The Shackles just activated its engines. And... damn it. The Assiduous just entered the system. Draco is here. Get to the hangar. If Varga launches, we lose everything."
scenes.gmNotes: Fioro reveals Varga's location and the Shackles of Nizon's status. Varth confirms Draco has entered the system. The heroes must flee the crime scene and race through Cloud City to the industrial docks before Varga escapes. This is a transition scene — setting up Part 3.

npcs.name: Lady Fioro
npcs.notes: Terrified. Provides critical intel under pressure.

passive.name: Ice Queen
passive.description: Immune to [Rattled] and [Shaken]. Fioro doesn't crack under pressure.

gambit.name: Reveal Identity
gambit.description: Invoke Hapan nobility. Guards defer. Civilians back away.

exploit.name: Insurance
exploit.description: If captured, reveal Varga's location in exchange for freedom.

threatBuild.notes: Varga's proxy. Not a fighter. Her value is the credits she holds and the information she'll spill under pressure. (Recurring — wounded, providing intel under duress.)

scenes.hazards: Wing Guard closing in. Imperial Star Destroyer entering the system. Time pressure.

decisions.consequence: Sprint through a city in lockdown. Leads to Part 3.

narrativeLinks.note: Direct transition to The Hangar Bay Showdown.

environmentMechanics.name: City in Lockdown
environmentMechanics.effect: Wing Guard deploying. Streets becoming dangerous. Heroes are persons of interest.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Move fast. Use back routes. Ugnaught allies can provide guidance.

rewards.narrative: Varga's location confirmed. Race to the docks begins.

pacing.risingAction: Intel confirmed. Shackles activating. Draco entering the system.

disciplineChallenges.context: Reading Fioro — is she telling the truth about Varga's location?

control.failure: Varth's logic seems sound. 'It's the only way.' No reason to doubt the tactical assessment.
control.mastery: You see through Varth completely: he knew Maya was on that ship. He knew before the escape started. He said nothing to the crew and was going to let her die. This isn't tactics — it's calculated silence.

effect.fleeting: Something about Varth's plan doesn't sit right. He dismissed the alternative too quickly.
effect.masterful: Varth knew Maya was on that ship before the escape started. He never mentioned it. Her death would have been convenient — one less person who knows what he's really after.
effect.legendary: Complete insight: Varth knew Maya went to the slave ship on her own. He could have told the crew. He could have warned her. He chose silence. If she dies, it's not his fault — she made her choice. That's how he sleeps at night.

disciplineChallenges.context: Sprint through Cloud City to the industrial docks.

control.failure: Delayed by traffic, crowds, or Wing Guard. Lose a critical round. Varga gains escape time.
control.mastery: Sprint so fast you arrive at the industrial docks with time to set up an ambush position before Varga's loading crew expects trouble.

effect.fleeting: Pushing through crowds. Slow going. Arrive winded but on time.
effect.masterful: Fast transit through service corridors. Arrive before Varga launches. Full team in position.
effect.legendary: You know Cloud City's shortcuts now. Underground tram to the industrial sector, maintenance elevator to the docks. Arrive fresh, fast, and undetected.

disciplineChallenges.context: Verifying Fioro's intel against what Varth can see from the ship.

control.failure: Can't verify independently. Trust her or don't. The clock is ticking.
control.mastery: Confirmed AND you discover additional detail Fioro didn't know: Varga has a decoy shuttle launching from a different bay. You know which one is real.

effect.fleeting: Varth's sensors detect engine activity at the dock Fioro described. Consistent but not conclusive.
effect.masterful: Confirmed: Shackles of Nizon is activating engines. Varga is loading cargo at Bay 4414. Fioro's intel checks out.
effect.legendary: Full verification: Fioro is right about everything. Additionally, Varth identifies Varga's security deployment — you know exactly how many guards and where they're positioned.

scenes.title: The Gauntlet
scenes.subtitle: Streets of Cloud City
scenes.readAloud:
The journey to the lower docks is a sprint through a city in lockdown. Varga has bribed the Wing Guard to declare you 'Enemy Saboteurs.'

The chase transitions from pristine white corridors into the steam-filled, rusted catwalks of the Industrial District. Tibanna gas vents hiss, obscuring vision, and the orange glow of the Bespin sunset filters through heavy industrial grates.

Varth hacks door controls from the ship: "Left at the junction! No, the other left! I'm jamming their comms, but I can't stop them from shooting at you. Move!"
scenes.gmNotes: Running battle through Cloud City. The heroes are pinned down by corrupt Wing Guard and Stormtroopers. This is a chase — stopping to fight every enemy means being overwhelmed. Use the environment: shoot steam pipes to scald pursuers, jump onto moving cargo skiffs, cut through maintenance ducts. Varth acts as 'The Man in the Chair,' hacking door controls.

npcs.name: Elite Bespin Security Guards
npcs.notes: Bribed by Varga. Hunting the heroes.

npcEffects.legendary: Chassis damage + [Suppressed] to all enemies in the target's zone

maneuver.name: Dig In
maneuver.description: Modifies Take Cover. Anchor gains [Cover 2] and [Optimized] on its next ranged attack. Cannot move this turn.

gambit.name: Pinning Barrage
gambit.description: In addition to normal effects, target gains [Suppressed] Tactical. Target cannot use Move next turn.

exploit.name: Covering Fire
exploit.trigger: An ally in the Anchor's zone or an adjacent zone takes damage.
exploit.description: Immediately make an attack at -2 Power against the attacker if in weapon range.

threatBuild.notes: Corrupt Wing Guard. Better trained than street thugs. Bribed by Varga to hunt the heroes.

npcs.name: Stormtrooper Squad
npcs.notes: Deployed from a shuttle to cut off retreat.

npcEffects.legendary: Chassis damage + [Suppressed] to all enemies in the target's zone

maneuver.name: Dig In
maneuver.description: Modifies Take Cover. Anchor gains [Cover 2] and [Optimized] on its next ranged attack. Cannot move this turn.

exploit.description: Immediately make an attack at -2 Power against the attacker if in weapon range.

threatBuild.notes: Standard Stormtrooper minions. Same stat block across adventures.

scenes.hazards: City lockdown. Steam pipes. Narrow catwalks. Corrupt law enforcement. Must keep moving or be overwhelmed.

decisions.consequence: Faster. Steam pipes, cargo skiffs, and maintenance ducts as escape tools.
Slower. Risks being overwhelmed by reinforcements.

encounters.name: The City Chase
encounters.trigger: Heroes flee the casino toward the docks
encounters.description: Running battle through Cloud City's streets and Industrial District. Wing Guard and Stormtroopers in pursuit.
encounters.tactics: Pursuit, not a stand-up fight. Each round, heroes must choose: fight or run. Standing still means more enemies arrive.

environmentMechanics.name: Steam Pipes
environmentMechanics.effect: Shoot pipes to create steam walls — [Concealment] for 2 rounds. Pursuers must slow down.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: N/A — tactical advantage for heroes.

environmentMechanics.name: Cargo Skiffs
environmentMechanics.trigger: If heroes reach a dock area
environmentMechanics.effect: Moving cargo platforms. Can jump aboard for rapid transport but must make Athletics check.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Athletics or fall between platforms.

environmentMechanics.name: Varth's Hacking
environmentMechanics.effect: Varth remotely opens/closes doors. Each success buys 1 round. Failures mean jammed doors.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Heroes can assist with Tech checks.

rewards.narrative: Arrive at Docking Bay 4414 ahead of (or just behind) the pursuit.

pacing.openingBeat: Sprint from the casino. Wing Guard declares them enemy saboteurs.
pacing.risingAction: Through pristine corridors into rusted catwalks. Steam and gunfire.
pacing.climax: The final stretch — must lose the pursuit before reaching the hangar.

disciplineChallenges.context: Dodging blaster fire while sprinting through corridors.

control.failure: Hit by glancing fire. Minor damage. Keep running — stopping means dying.
control.mastery: You dodge so cleanly that the pursuing shooters hit each other's cover positions. Friendly fire chaos buys you an extra round.

effect.fleeting: Bolt singes your jacket. Close call. Keep moving.
effect.masterful: Clean dodge. Keep running. Not a scratch.
effect.legendary: Inhuman reflexes. You anticipate the shots before they fire — ducking and weaving through a corridor of blaster bolts without breaking stride.

disciplineChallenges.context: Parkour through the Industrial District — leaping gaps, climbing pipes.

control.failure: Stumble on a pipe. Pursuers close the gap. Must fight or find another route.
control.mastery: You gain so much distance that the pursuers lose visual contact. They're searching blind while you reach the hangar unimpeded.

effect.fleeting: Awkward climb over a steam pipe. Slow but you don't fall. Pursuers are still behind you.
effect.masterful: Fluid movement. Leap gaps, climb pipes, vault railings. Gain a round on pursuers.
effect.legendary: Parkour through the industrial district like you've done it your whole life. Pursuers can't keep up — you're using routes they can't follow.

disciplineChallenges.context: Assisting Varth's remote door hacking.

control.failure: Unfamiliar Hutt technology. The yacht's systems respond sluggishly.
control.mastery: Full system mastery AND you discover Varga's personal encrypted files in the yacht's computer. Intelligence goldmine accessible after the battle.

effect.fleeting: Basic systems operational. Weapons and shields respond, but the yacht handles like a barge.
effect.masterful: Full system integration. The yacht responds crisply. Shields, weapons, and engines all performing at spec.
effect.legendary: You coax performance out of the Chariot that Varga never knew it had. Overclocked engines, boosted shields, and a weapons system that hits harder than its spec sheet says.

disciplineChallenges.context: Slipping through a checkpoint undetected.

control.failure: Spotted. Reinforcements called. The checkpoint becomes a bottleneck.
control.mastery: Clean passage AND you lock the checkpoint behind you — pursuers hit a sealed door. Major delay for them.

effect.fleeting: Timing is tight. You slip past during a guard change but it was close. One more second and you'd have been seen.
effect.masterful: Guards don't see you. Clean passage through the checkpoint.
effect.legendary: You pass through the checkpoint like a ghost. The guards never even look in your direction. You're through and invisible.

disciplineChallenges.context: Suppressive fire to cover the group's movement.

control.failure: Shots miss. TIEs are too fast. Must get closer or find a better angle.
control.mastery: Shot hits the TIE's solar panel junction — instant kill. Debris forces the wingman to break formation.

effect.fleeting: Glancing hit on a TIE's wing. It wobbles but stays in the fight. Damaged but operational.
effect.masterful: Clean hit. TIE spirals away trailing smoke. One less fighter in the pursuit.
effect.legendary: Devastating shot. The TIE detonates — and the explosion catches the wingman's solar panel. Two for one. The pursuit thins dramatically.

scenes.title: Hutt's Hangar
scenes.subtitle: Docking Bay 4414
scenes.readAloud:
You kick open the blast doors to Docking Bay 4414. The cavernous hangar is open to the roiling clouds of Bespin on one side, wind howling through the magnetic field.

Dominating the center is Varga's personal luxury yacht — the Glorious Chariot. Gold and purple, engines whining with a turbine squeal that vibrates in your teeth. Six Z-95 Headhunters idle on the deck, pilots scrambling into cockpits.

Varga stands at the base of the ramp, screaming at his loading crews. When he sees you, fear becomes rage.

"Kill them! They brought the Inquisitor down on us! I want their heads!"
scenes.gmNotes: The final confrontation with Varga. The Glorious Chariot is priming for launch. Six Z-95 fighters are scrambling — if they get airborne, they'll strafe the hangar. Savvy heroes might shoot pilots on ladders to hijack fighters. Varga retreats up the ramp as mercenaries fall. Heroes must breach the ship or disable its engines. If they board, they fight Varga in the yacht's narrow corridors where his massive bulk is a weapon.

npcs.name: Varga the Hutt
npcs.notes: Desperate and enraged. Will fight with his bulk in the yacht's corridors.

npcEffects.masterful: Chassis damage + one ally in the same zone heals 1 Vitality
npcEffects.legendary: Chassis damage + ally heals 2 Vitality + remove one condition from that ally

gambit.name: Rally

exploit.name: Emergency Aid
exploit.trigger: An ally in the same zone is reduced to 0 Vitality.
exploit.description: That ally is instead reduced to 1 Vitality. Usable once per scene.

Direct combat is a mistake — he's a Hutt on a hover-sled surrounded by guards. (Recurring — same stat block from Adv2. Now desperate and enraged.)

npcs.name: Z-95 Pilots
npcs.notes: Scrambling to cockpits. Must be stopped before takeoff.

npcEffects.legendary: Chassis damage + [Suppressed] to all enemies in the target's zone

exploit.description: Immediately make an attack at -2 Power against the attacker if in weapon range.

npcs.name: Heavy Security Droid
npcs.notes: Flanking Varga at the ramp.

npcEffects.legendary: Chassis damage + [Staggered] + [Prone]

maneuver.description: Modifies Move. Move 1 zone toward a target. That target must succeed on a Grit check vs Threat's Power or gain [Rattled].

scenes.hazards: Open hangar to Bespin's clouds. Z-95s attempting takeoff. Fuel crates. Magnetic field boundary.

Risk being strafed if fighters launch, but could end the fight faster.

encounters.name: Hangar Bay Assault
encounters.description: Varga's yacht prepping for launch. 6 Z-95s scrambling. Security droid and thugs defending.
encounters.tactics: Varga screams orders from the ramp. Thugs create a screen. Z-95 pilots scramble. Security droid guards the yacht.

environmentMechanics.name: Z-95 Scramble
environmentMechanics.effect: 1 Z-95 launches per round unless pilots are stopped. Airborne Z-95s strafe the hangar.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Shoot pilots on ladders. Disable fighters with Tech. Hijack them.

environmentMechanics.name: Magnetic Field Boundary
environmentMechanics.effect: Open to Bespin's atmosphere. Characters pushed past the boundary fall.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Stay away from the edge. Or push enemies over it.

environmentMechanics.name: Fuel Crates
environmentMechanics.effect: [Hazard 3] explosion in zone. Major area denial.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Tactical positioning. Use explosions offensively.

rewards.narrative: Hangar contested. Z-95s prevented from launching (or hijacked). Varga on the yacht.

pacing.openingBeat: Blast doors open. The Glorious Chariot dominates the bay.
pacing.risingAction: Z-95s scrambling. Security droid advancing. Thugs opening fire.
pacing.climax: Reach the yacht before Varga seals the ramp.
pacing.resolution: Hangar control established (or not). The firefight continues on the ship.

disciplineChallenges.context: Stopping Z-95 pilots from reaching their cockpits.

control.failure: Pilots reach cockpits. Z-95s launching in 2 rounds. Air superiority lost.
control.mastery: Pilots pinned down AND you disable a Z-95's engines with precision shots to the intake. One fewer fighter to deal with in the air.

effect.fleeting: Suppressive fire slows the pilots. They're behind cover but still moving toward the fighters. Bought 1 round.
effect.masterful: Pilots pinned down. Fighters don't launch this round. Window secured for the ship grab.
effect.legendary: All pilots pinned or wounded. Not a single Z-95 is going airborne without someone crawling through a firefight to reach a cockpit.

disciplineChallenges.context: Hijacking a Z-95 Headhunter.

control.failure: Unfamiliar controls. Delayed 1 round while you figure out the interface.
control.mastery: Instant startup AND you discover the Z-95 has been modified with concussion missiles. Bonus armament for the escape.

effect.fleeting: Basic systems online. The fighter will fly but you're learning the controls in real time.
effect.masterful: Engine start. You're flying. Weapons hot. Firepower for the escape.
effect.legendary: Complete system mastery in seconds. You have the fighter singing — weapons, shields, comms, and a targeting computer that locks fast.

disciplineChallenges.context: Identifying the priority targets in the hangar.

control.failure: Chaotic scene. React to threats as they emerge. No priority targeting.
control.mastery: Perfect threat assessment AND you identify the hangar's emergency seal controls. If things go wrong, you can seal the hangar and trap everyone inside — including yourselves.

effect.fleeting: Identify immediate threats: armed guards, the security droid, and pilots running for fighters.
effect.masterful: Priority list: stop the yacht engines, neutralize the security droid, prevent fighter launches. Clear tactical picture.
effect.legendary: Complete battle plan: assignments for each team member, firing arcs that avoid damaging the ships you need, and a 30-second timeline that ends with you airborne.

disciplineChallenges.context: Sprinting across the hangar to reach the Glorious Chariot.

control.failure: Intercepted by thugs. Delayed. Must fight through before reaching the yacht.
control.mastery: Sprint so fast you reach the ramp and get inside before Varga's crew can seal it. You're aboard and they're locked out.

effect.fleeting: Dodging between cargo and fuel lines. Slow going under fire.
effect.masterful: Full speed. Reach the ramp before Varga seals it. Clean entry.
effect.legendary: Sprint-vault-slide across the hangar floor. You cover 50 meters of open ground under fire without being hit, hit the ramp at full speed, and are inside the yacht in seconds.

scenes.title: The Firefight
scenes.readAloud:
The hangar erupts into chaos. Blaster bolts ricochet off hull plating and fuel crates. The Z-95 engines roar to life one by one. Varga, seeing his mercenaries fall, turns and lumbers up the boarding ramp of the Glorious Chariot.

The yacht's engines begin their warm-up cycle. If he seals that ramp, he's gone.
scenes.gmNotes: The climactic ground fight. Heroes must cross the hangar using fuel crates and machinery for cover. Primary objective: stop the Glorious Chariot from launching. If Z-95s get airborne, they strafe the hangar floor. If heroes breach the yacht, they fight Varga in opulent but narrow corridors. Varga fights desperately — he knows this is his last stand.

items.note: Project Leviathan files, shipping records, Imperial contacts.

rewards.narrative: Varga defeated. The Glorious Chariot is captured. Act I climax approaching.

pacing.openingBeat: The hangar erupts. Blaster fire and engine roar.
pacing.risingAction: Varga retreats to the yacht. Mercs cover him.
pacing.climax: Boarding the yacht. Close-quarters fight with a Hutt.
pacing.resolution: Varga falls. The yacht is secured.

disciplineChallenges.context: Close-quarters combat with Varga in the yacht's corridors.

control.failure: Varga's bulk fills the corridor. He crushes you against the wall. [Stunned] for 1 round.
control.mastery: Devastating blow AND you sever the controls for the hover-sled. Varga's immobilized — a beached Hutt. Fight's over.

effect.fleeting: Blade connects but Varga's fat absorbs the worst of it. He bellows in pain and rage. Hurt but not stopped.
effect.masterful: Decisive blow. Varga takes heavy damage. The Hutt's bravado cracks — he's not used to being hurt.
effect.legendary: Critical strike to the hover-sled's motivator. Sparks shower. The sled crashes to the deck with Varga still on it. He's grounded and exposed.

disciplineChallenges.context: Surviving Varga's tail whip in tight quarters.

control.failure: Knocked into bulkhead. [Stunned] for 1 round. Stars in your eyes.
control.mastery: Not only absorb the blow but grab the tail. Varga's immobilized for 1 round — massive opening for allies.

effect.fleeting: The impact drives the air from your lungs. You stagger but stay on your feet. Everything hurts.
effect.masterful: Absorb the blow. Stay standing. Your body protests but your legs hold. Ready to counter.
effect.legendary: You catch the tail whip on your forearms and brace. Varga's surprised — no one has ever taken that hit standing. Your counter-attack has +1 Power.

disciplineChallenges.context: Disabling the Glorious Chariot's engines from inside.

control.failure: Engines spool up. Must be disabled from the cockpit — which Varga's crew controls.
control.mastery: Engines disabled AND you lock the cockpit remotely. Varga's pilot can't override. The yacht is permanently grounded without a full repair.

effect.fleeting: One engine shuts down. The yacht can still fly on the other three, but maneuvering is compromised.
effect.masterful: Engines shut down. The yacht isn't going anywhere. Primary objective secured.
effect.legendary: Complete power shutdown. Engines, shields, weapons, and life support all go dark. The Chariot is a very expensive metal box. Varga has nowhere to run.

disciplineChallenges.context: Clearing mercenaries from the hangar floor.

control.failure: Bodyguards maintain position. Varga stays shielded. Must reposition.
control.mastery: Two bodyguards drop in rapid succession. Varga's escort collapses. The Hutt is exposed and terrified.

effect.fleeting: One bodyguard takes cover but the formation holds. Varga's protection is thinning but not broken.
effect.masterful: Clean shots. One bodyguard down, another wounded. The protective ring around Varga is breaking.
effect.legendary: Devastating volley. The bodyguard formation shatters. Varga's hover-sled has no cover. The Hutt begins bellowing in Huttese — pure panic.

scenes.title: Grand Theft Starship
scenes.readAloud:
With Varga defeated and the hangar secured, you barely have time to breathe before Varth barks over the comms:

"Imperial stormtroopers have breached the elevator shaft! You have thirty seconds before a platoon comes through that door. You can't go back the way you came. Take the ships! Steal the yacht! I'll rendezvous with you in orbit!"
scenes.gmNotes: The heroes commandeer the Glorious Chariot and any intact Z-95 Headhunters. As hangar doors blast open and Stormtroopers pour in, the heroes punch the throttles and blast out of the hangar bay into the orange clouds of Bespin. This transitions to the orbital battle.

npcs.notes: Breaching the elevator shaft. 30 seconds until arrival.

npcEffects.legendary: Chassis damage + [Suppressed] to all enemies in the target's zone
