scenes.hazards: 30-second countdown. Stormtrooper platoon incoming. Must launch immediately.

decisions.consequence: Luxury yacht with decent shields and weapons. The primary escape vehicle.
Additional firepower for the orbital battle. Requires pilots.

encounters.name: 30-Second Countdown
encounters.description: Heroes have 30 seconds (5 rounds) to launch before 20 Stormtroopers breach. Hot-start the yacht and fighters, then blast out.
encounters.tactics: No time to fight. Every action must go toward launching. If not airborne by round 5, overwhelming force arrives.

environmentMechanics.name: 30-Second Clock
environmentMechanics.effect: Each round is 6 seconds. Round 5 = Stormtrooper platoon breaches. Round 6+ = overwhelmed.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Focus entirely on launch preparations. No side tasks.

items.name: The Glorious Chariot
items.note: Varga's luxury yacht. Now the heroes' ship.

items.name: Z-95 Headhunters
items.note: Up to 4 hijacked fighters (depending on how many were stopped from launching).

rewards.narrative: Airborne. Breaking atmosphere. The orbital battle begins.

pacing.openingBeat: Varth screams: "Stormtroopers! 30 seconds! TAKE THE SHIPS!"
pacing.climax: Blast doors opening. White armor pouring in.
pacing.resolution: Engines fire. The yacht punches through the magnetic field into orange clouds.

disciplineChallenges.context: Hot-starting the Glorious Chariot under fire.

control.failure: Engine sputter. Delayed 1 round. Stormtroopers breach the hangar. Under fire during startup.
control.mastery: Instant hot-start AND you use the yacht's bulk to block the hangar entrance. The Stormtroopers can't get a clean shot.

effect.fleeting: Engines cough once, then catch. Slow lift. The yacht clears the pad with Stormtroopers firing at the hull.
effect.masterful: Engines roar. The yacht lifts off immediately. Clean departure under pressure.
effect.legendary: The Chariot launches like it was built for combat ops. You use the repulsor wash to scatter the Stormtrooper formation and blast through the hangar doors before they can seal.

disciplineChallenges.context: Activating unfamiliar Z-95 fighter systems.

control.failure: Unknown control layout. -1 Power on first piloting check. Systems fight you.
control.mastery: Full system mastery on first contact. AND you discover the Z-95s have been upgraded with military-grade targeting computers. +1 Power on ranged attacks.

effect.fleeting: Basic systems online. Weapons cycle. The fighter is flyable but you're learning the cockpit on the fly.
effect.masterful: Weapons hot. Systems green. Ready to fly. The Z-95 responds to your inputs cleanly.
effect.legendary: You crack the Z-95's systems in seconds. Full weapons, shields, and — bonus — an encrypted IFF transponder that reads as Imperial. Useful for bluffing past patrols later.

disciplineChallenges.context: Deciding which ships to take — yacht, fighters, or both.

control.failure: Rushed decision. May leave useful assets behind. Regret later.
control.mastery: Optimal loadout AND you identify a way to remote-slave the unmanned fighters. Three ships controlled by two pilots.

effect.fleeting: Grab what's closest. One ship for the party, leave the rest. Speed over strategy.
effect.masterful: Optimal loadout identified. Maximum firepower for the escape — yacht plus fighters for escort.
effect.legendary: Complete tactical assessment: which ships to take, which to sabotage so they can't pursue, and the ideal formation for the escape run.

scenes.title: Skirmish Over Bespin
scenes.readAloud:
You break atmosphere and the sensor board lights up. The Imperial Star Destroyer Assiduous hangs in high orbit, launching TIE Fighters. The Shackles of Nizon — a transport filled with slaves — drifts dead in space. A massive automated Tibanna Gas Hauler is burning hard on a collision course with the slave ship.

Varth's voice crackles from the Banshee, cold and professional:

"Good work getting airborne. That Gas Hauler is going to impact the transport in less than two minutes. It's a tragedy, but it's not our tragedy. We have the credits, we have the data. Spool up the hyperdrives. Let's get out of here while we're rich."

Then a frantic transmission cuts through:

"Varth! I'm on the transport! The slave circuits are locked! I can't steer and sensors show collision imminent! Where is that backup code?!"

It's Maya.
scenes.gmNotes: The moral climax of Act I. Maya went to the Shackles of Nizon on her own — she couldn't let the slaves die after Varth refused to help. Varth knew she went and deliberately did not tell the crew. He's aboard the Banshee, safely out of the blast zone. The Empire is using the Gas Hauler as a ram to destroy the Shackles of Nizon. Varth's position: 'She made her choice. We jump. Now.' The heroes must choose: follow Varth and leave Maya to die, or turn and fight. If they fight, they engage TIE Fighters to destroy the Gas Hauler. Varth eventually helps — not from altruism, but because he needs the heroes alive for the fortune.

npcs.name: Maya
npcs.notes: Trapped on the Shackles of Nizon. She went on her own to free the slaves after Varth refused. Varth knew and never told the crew.

scenes.hazards: Star Destroyer in orbit. TIE Fighter screen. Automated Gas Hauler on collision course. Two-minute countdown.

decisions.choice: Turn and fight to save Maya
decisions.consequence: Engage TIEs and destroy the Gas Hauler. Varth reluctantly helps. Heroic but dangerous.

decisions.choice: Follow Varth's order and jump to hyperspace
decisions.consequence: Maya dies. The slaves die. The heroes are rich but compromised.

narrativeLinks.note: Maya has been the crew's pilot and closest ally since the beginning. She went to the slave ship because she couldn't stand by. Varth knew and said nothing — his silence reveals his true nature.

encounters.name: Orbital Battle Over Bespin
encounters.description: TIE Fighter screen between the heroes and the Gas Hauler. 2-minute countdown to collision with the slave ship. Star Destroyer in the background.
encounters.tactics: TIEs screen the Hauler. Must punch through or go around. The Hauler is automated — no pilot to negotiate with. Must be destroyed before impact.

environmentMechanics.name: Collision Timer
environmentMechanics.effect: 2-minute countdown (20 rounds). When it hits 0, the Hauler rams the Shackles of Nizon. Everyone aboard dies.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Destroy the Hauler. Or transmit the slave circuit code so Maya can steer clear.

environmentMechanics.name: Star Destroyer Presence
environmentMechanics.effect: The Assiduous fires turbolaser batteries at long range. Random [Hazard 2] in one zone per round.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Don't stay in one zone. Keep moving.

environmentMechanics.name: Bespin's Atmosphere
environmentMechanics.effect: Tibanna gas clouds provide cover but also turbulence. Piloting check or [Disoriented].
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Stay above the cloud layer unless desperate.

rewards.narrative: Gas Hauler destroyed. Maya saved (or abandoned). The moral choice that defines Act I.

pacing.openingBeat: Break atmosphere. Sensors light up. TIEs, Star Destroyer, and a collision countdown.
pacing.risingAction: TIE Fighter dogfight. Racing to reach the Gas Hauler.
pacing.climax: The shot — destroy the Hauler before it rams the slave ship.
pacing.resolution: Explosion. Maya's voice: "Slave circuits fried. We're jumping."

disciplineChallenges.context: Dogfighting TIE Fighters while racing to destroy the Gas Hauler.

control.failure: TIEs on your tail. Taking fire. Hull integrity dropping. Must shake them before attempting the Hauler run.
control.mastery: Ace maneuver — you use a TIE's own wingman as a shield, forcing one to break off to avoid friendly fire. Clear run on the Hauler.

effect.fleeting: Evasive flying keeps you alive but you can't get a clean approach on the Hauler. Need to deal with the TIEs first.
effect.masterful: Outmaneuver the TIEs. Clear firing line on the Hauler. One clean pass available.
effect.legendary: Thread the needle between two TIEs at full speed, leaving them in each other's wake turbulence. Perfect attack run lined up on the Gas Hauler.

disciplineChallenges.context: Destroying the automated Gas Hauler before it rams the slave ship.

control.failure: Glancing hit. Hauler is damaged but still on collision course. Need another shot — and another round you might not have.
control.mastery: Hit the fuel line junction. The Hauler doesn't just break apart — it detonates spectacularly, creating a debris field that forces the pursuing TIEs to scatter.

effect.fleeting: Solid hit to the hull. The Hauler's course wobbles. It's slowing but still moving toward the Shackles. Not enough.
effect.masterful: Direct hit. The Hauler breaks apart. Maya is safe. Debris scatters harmlessly.
effect.legendary: Precision shot to the engine block. The Hauler disintegrates cleanly — no debris threat to the Shackles. Maya whoops over the comm. Perfect kill.

disciplineChallenges.context: Transmitting the slave circuit code to free the Shackles of Nizon.

control.failure: Transmission interference from the Star Destroyer. Must get closer — within turbolaser range.
control.mastery: Circuits unlocked AND you piggyback a virus onto the signal that corrupts the Star Destroyer's slave circuit database. They can never re-enslave this frequency band.

effect.fleeting: Partial transmission. Some circuits unlock. Maya has partial helm control — enough to maneuver but not full power.
effect.masterful: Circuits unlocked. Maya regains full helm control. The Shackles breaks free of its programmed course.
effect.legendary: Clean unlock with a bonus: the transmission overloads the Iron Ring's tracking beacon on the ship. The Shackles is not only free but untraceable.

disciplineChallenges.context: Resisting Varth's pressure to jump and leave Maya.

control.failure: Varth's logic is compelling. Hesitation costs a round while you argue internally.
control.mastery: You defy Varth AND rally the crew. Everyone commits to the rescue. Varth is overruled by unanimous decision — and he sees the writing on the wall.

effect.fleeting: You push back but the doubt lingers. 'What if he's right?' A moment of uncertainty before committing.
effect.masterful: You defy Varth. Your ship, your choice. No hesitation. Maya lives.
effect.legendary: You don't just defy Varth — you redefine the mission. 'We're not the kind of people who leave someone to die.' The crew follows your lead. Varth is silent.

scenes.title: The Choice
scenes.subtitle: End of Act I
scenes.readAloud:
The Tibanna Gas Hauler explodes in a massive fireball just meters from the slave ship. Maya's voice cuts through the static, shaking with relief: "Slave circuits fried. I have helm control. Jumping to Takodana."

The Assiduous opens fire with turbolasers. Green bolts flash past your cockpit. Varth screams from the Banshee:

"We overstayed our welcome! PUNCH IT!"

The stars stretch into lines and you're gone.

You drop out of hyperspace over Takodana. Green forests and silver lakes fill the viewport below — and hanging in low orbit, engines dark, is the Shackles of Nizon. A massive Imperial bulk transport, its hull scarred and ugly, cargo bays built for three hundred bodies in chains. The slave circuits are dark. The running lights are on.

Maya got here first.
scenes.gmNotes:
The aftermath of Act I. The crew arrives at Takodana to find the Shackles of Nizon already in orbit — Maya jumped here ahead of them. The bulk transport is too large to land; it sits in low orbit while Maya shuttles freed slaves down to Maz Kanata's castle. Denia is already planetside — she's been recovering at Maz's since the end of Adventure 2. The Banshee is docked at the castle.

When the crew lands at Maz's castle, Maya is waiting. She walks straight to Varth and punches him in the jaw. He takes it. 'She made her choice. And you made yours.' Varth doesn't apologize — he never does.

Rewards: The Glorious Chariot, Z-95 fighters, tournament winnings, Varga's assets, Project Leviathan files. Varth unlocks the encrypted account and pays the crew their cut.

Varth's pivot: 'This data points to a massive construction project in the Unknown Regions. If you want to know what the Empire is really building, we have to go off the map.' He looks at Denia: 'How did the Jedi navigate this part of space?' Denia suggests the Ebon Spire on the forest moon of Endor. Varth nods like he's hearing it for the first time.

This concludes Act I and sets up the campaign's second act. Takodana — Maz's castle — is the crew's home base.

threatBuild.notes: The crew's pilot since the beginning. Went to the Shackles of Nizon on her own to free the slaves. Trapped when slave circuits locked. Varth knew she went and said nothing to the crew.

scenes.hazards: Turbolaser fire from the Assiduous. Must jump to hyperspace immediately.

decisions.consequence: He deflects — 'tactical calculation.' But the heroes now know what he's capable of.

narrativeLinks.note: The argument the players overheard. Maya said she wanted to help the slaves. Varth said no. She went anyway. And now here she is — with three hundred freed people and a punch for Varth.

rewards.narrative: Act I concludes. The heroes have Varga's fortune, his ship, and critical intelligence. But they also know what Varth is capable of. The campaign shifts from "survive and profit" to "what is the Empire building in the Unknown Regions?"

pacing.openingBeat: The Gas Hauler explodes. Maya jumps to Takodana. The Assiduous opens fire.
pacing.risingAction: Hyperspace jump under turbolaser fire. Drop out over Takodana — the Shackles of Nizon is already in orbit. Maya got here first.
pacing.climax: At Maz's castle: Maya punches Varth.
pacing.resolution: Act I ends. Varth teases Act II: "If you want to know what the Empire is really building, we have to go off the map."

disciplineChallenges.context: Jumping to hyperspace under turbolaser fire.

control.failure: Glancing turbolaser hit during jump. Ship takes damage but enters hyperspace. Repairs needed.
control.mastery: Clean jump AND you enter hyperspace on a vector that makes pursuit impossible — the Star Destroyer can't follow without recalculating. Total escape.

effect.fleeting: Hull shudders from a near-miss. The navicomputer locks and you punch it. Stars stretch — but the ship groans.
effect.masterful: Clean jump. Stars stretch into lines. The turbolaser fire flashes harmlessly behind you.
effect.legendary: Perfect microjump timing. You enter hyperspace in the exact gap between turbolaser salvos. Not a scratch on the hull. Textbook.

disciplineChallenges.context: Reading Varth after his silence nearly killed Maya. What is he really?

control.failure: His explanation seems reasonable — 'tactical calculation.' But something doesn't sit right. You can't articulate why.
control.mastery: You see through everything. Varth isn't just calculating — he's rehearsed. He expected this confrontation and prepared responses. He's been managing you from the beginning.

effect.fleeting: Cold. He's processing, not feeling. The mathematical precision of his response is unsettling.
effect.masterful: Cold. Calculating. He didn't hesitate to sacrifice her. He will do it again if the math works. This is who he is.
effect.legendary: Complete psychological profile: Varth views people as assets to be deployed and expended. His charm is a tool. His loyalty is conditional. Every relationship is a transaction — including this one.

disciplineChallenges.context: Confronting Varth about knowing Maya was on the slave ship and saying nothing.

control.failure: Varth deflects cleanly. 'Tactical calculation that changed. You made a different call. I respect that.' Smooth as durasteel.
control.mastery: Varth drops the mask entirely. A flash of genuine emotion — not remorse, but frustration. 'You don't understand what's at stake.' He almost says too much. Almost reveals the bigger picture.

effect.fleeting: Varth pauses. Considers your words. Then: 'Noted.' That's all you get. The wall is up.
effect.masterful: Varth drops the mask for a moment. 'I did what I thought was necessary. I was wrong.' Genuine or calculated? Hard to tell — but the crack is real.
effect.legendary: Varth is genuinely shaken. For one unguarded moment, you see the real man behind the Admiral's bearing — someone who knows he's becoming something he despises. The moment passes. But you saw it.

disciplineChallenges.context: Processing the emotional weight of Act I's events.

control.failure: [Rattled] carries into the next adventure. The weight lingers. Sleepless nights.
control.mastery: Not just processing — transforming. The events of Bespin forge a harder, clearer version of yourself. Begin the next adventure with renewed purpose.

effect.fleeting: You compartmentalize. File it away. Not healthy, but functional. You can operate.
effect.masterful: Steel yourself. Ready for whatever comes next. The experiences of Act I have made you stronger.
effect.legendary: Clarity. Everything that happened — Raden's death, Maya's rescue, Varth's mask slipping — it all resolves into purpose. You know what you're fighting for now. Nothing can shake that.
